title: Logistics Industry Veteran Andrew Clarke Named Chairman of Global Critical Logistics
text: LOS ANGELES--(BUSINESS WIRE)--The board of directors of Global Critical Logistics (“GCL”), a leading provider of mission-critical freight forwarding and global logistics providers to specialty sectors, announced today that logistics industry veteran Andrew Clarke, has been named Chairman. Mr. Clarke joined GCL's board in 2018. title: Alphabet's Waymo, CH Robinson to test automated trucks in Texas
text: Alphabet Inc's self-driving technology unit Waymo and truck fleet operator C.H. Robinson Worldwide Inc said on Wednesday they would team up to test automated trucks with safety drivers behind the wheels in Texas in the coming months.

title: Read How Analysts Reacted To CH Robinson Worldwide's Q4 Results
text: KeyBanc analyst Todd Fowler lowered CH Robinson Worldwide Inc's  CHRW  price target to $125 (an upside of 38%) from $135 and maintained an Overweight rating on the shares. While gross profit margins lagged his expectations, the analyst continues to anticipate margin improvement throughout 2022 as contract rates reset.

title: C.H. Robinson (CHRW) Loses 15.6% in 4 Weeks, Here's Why a Trend Reversal May be Around the Corner

title: C.H. Robinson (CHRW) Q4 Earnings Miss, Revenues Up 43% Y/Y
text: C.H. Robinson's (CHRW) Q4 results are hurt by high operating expenses. However, improved pricing and volumes across most service lines boost revenues.

title: CH Robinson Worldwide Stock Plunges After Q4 EPS Miss
text: CH Robinson Worldwide Inc  CHRW reported fourth-quarter revenue growth of 42.9% year-over-year to $6.5 billion, beating the consensus of $6.22 billion. Revenue increase was driven primarily by higher pricing and higher volume across most of the company's services.

title: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) Q4 Earnings Miss Estimates
text: C.H. Robinson (CHRW) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of -5.95% and 5.03%, respectively, for the quarter ended December 2021. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: C.H. Robinson's earnings miss high expectations as operating expenses spike
text: Leading North American third-party logistics provider C.H. Robinson (NASDAQ: CHRW ) reported fourth-quarter adjusted earnings per share of $1.74 before the markets opened on Wednesday, up 61% year-over-year but missing consensus estimates of $1.85 per share.
